sections.title: Desk, chair & monitor
sections.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' width='120' height='120' aria-hidden='true' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='14' y='20' width='40' height='26' rx='3' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5'/><line x1='34' y1='46' x2='34' y2='56' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5'/><line x1='22' y1='56' x2='46' y2='56' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5'/><circle cx='78' cy='30' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#d98a3d' stroke-width='2.5'/><path d='M78 37 v18 M78 44 h14 M78 55 l-6 12 M78 55 l8 12' fill='none' stroke='#d98a3d' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='66' y1='86' x2='104' y2='86' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/></svg>
tips: Set the screen so the top of it sits at or just below eye level, about an arm's length away.
Keep elbows close to your body and bent near 90°, with forearms level when typing.
Use a chair that supports the natural inward curve of your lower back, or add a small cushion.
Rest both feet flat on the floor (or a footrest) with hips slightly higher than knees.
Bring the keyboard and mouse close so you don't have to reach forward and round your shoulders.

sections.title: Sitting habits
sections.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' width='120' height='120' aria-hidden='true' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='52' cy='28' r='8' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5'/><path d='M52 36 C52 50 52 58 52 64' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M52 64 h22' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M74 64 v22' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M52 46 l16 8' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M40 92 q24 -10 48 0' fill='none' stroke='#d98a3d' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/></svg>
tips: Avoid staying in one position too long — change posture or stand up at least every 30 minutes.
Sit back fully so the chair supports you, rather than perching on the front edge.
Keep your weight even on both hips instead of leaning to one side or crossing your legs.
Take short walking or stretching breaks; gentle movement nourishes the discs in your spine.
Set a quiet reminder to do a posture reset and roll your shoulders back a few times a day.

sections.title: Safe lifting
sections.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' width='120' height='120' aria-hidden='true' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='42' cy='26' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5'/><path d='M42 33 C46 44 48 50 52 56' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M52 56 l-6 18' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M46 74 l-2 14' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M44 42 l16 18' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><rect x='56' y='60' width='22' height='22' rx='2' fill='none' stroke='#d98a3d' stroke-width='2.5'/><line x1='34' y1='94' x2='90' y2='94' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/></svg>
tips: Hinge at your hips and bend your knees to lower yourself — let your legs do the lifting, not your back.
Keep the load close to your body and centred between your feet before you stand.
Brace your stomach gently and keep your back in its natural line rather than rounding it.
Turn with your feet instead of twisting your spine while holding something.
If a load feels too heavy or awkward, ask for help or split it into smaller trips.

sections.title: Sleep positions
sections.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' width='120' height='120' aria-hidden='true' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='12' y1='90' x2='108' y2='90' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><rect x='18' y='66' width='22' height='14' rx='4' fill='none' stroke='#d98a3d' stroke-width='2.5'/><circle cx='34' cy='62' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5'/><path d='M40 70 q24 -2 40 10' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M62 78 l8 12 M70 80 l8 10' fill='none' stroke='#2f8273' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><ellipse cx='72' cy='84' rx='6' ry='4' fill='none' stroke='#d98a3d' stroke-width='2.5'/></svg>
tips: If you sleep on your side, place a pillow between your knees to keep your hips and spine aligned.
If you sleep on your back, try a pillow under your knees to ease the curve of your lower back.
Choose a pillow that keeps your neck level with your spine — not tilted up or dropped down.
Try to avoid sleeping on your stomach, which tends to flatten and twist the lower back.
Roll like a log — moving shoulders and hips together — when turning over or getting out of bed.

checklist.text: Set my screen to eye level before I start work
Stood up or moved at least once every 30 minutes
Sat back with my lower back supported
Used a hip hinge to lift anything heavy
Took a short walk or stretch break
Set up pillow support for sleep
